name: What are the most common mistakes people make when remodeling a bathroom?
headline: What are the most common mistakes people make when remodeling a bathroom?

mainEntity.name: What are the most common mistakes people make when remodeling a bathroom?
mainEntity.text: What are the most common mistakes people make when remodeling a bathroom?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common mistakes in bathroom remodels, especially in older Bay Area homes, usually come from rushing, under-budgeting, or ignoring moisture issues. One big mistake is skipping permits or inspections to save time or money. This can backfire later when you sell the home or if a problem like a leak shows up. Another issue is choosing cheap materials in wet areas. Thin, low-grade tile, basic grout, or poor-quality shower doors may fail quickly. It’s better to spend more on waterproofing, tile installation, and proper fans than on fancy faucets. People also often underestimate how long decisions take. Changing your mind on layouts or finishes once work starts can cause delays, extra cost, and conflicts. Try to finalize your plan and key materials before demo. Layout mistakes include squeezing in too large a vanity, making the shower opening too small, or not planning enough storage. Think about daily use: where towels go, where you plug in hair dryers, and how doors swing. Finally, not planning for a backup bathroom or daily routine is a common pitfall. If it’s your only bathroom, you need a clear plan for showers and toilet use during critical phases. Take time to plan, ask questions, and prioritize function and durability over trendy looks. Careful preparation helps you avoid most of these problems.
